readConfig normalizes dogfood_repos: string + object shapes → {path,label}, junk ignored, dead repo warned+skipped, absent → []\nPASS  frozenJudgeHits flags judge files changed outside the declared scope\nPASS  rankBacklog groups rows in-flight → proposed → done, stable within groups\nPASS  backlog badges render counts and refresh idempotently in README markers\nPASS  capture queue: add, pending, flush, and surfacing contracts\nPASS  feedback: SCHEMA_VERSION, ENTRY_FIELDS, DROP_REASONS pinned to their source literals (drift guard)\nPASS  feedback: source contains no bare fs.<read> call and no aliased node:fs read import (read-scope drift guard)\nPASS  feedback: resolveInScope returns a real absolute path, null when absent, and throws on every escape\nPASS  feedback: a symlinked cell escaping the repo is rejected by realpath containment, warned, and never read\nPASS  feedback: empty repo yields a valid zero-count snapshot without throwing (absent sources skipped + counted)\nPASS  feedback: the allowlist carries no free text — friction detail naming readBacklogCounts/COMMAND_KEYS never reaches the digest\nPASS  feedback: a secret in a title is dropped as a security event (scan runs BEFORE truncation), key absent from bytes\nPASS  feedback: an injection payload in a title is dropped as injection; dropped shape carries the category only\nPASS  feedback: kind vocabulary — review-finding maps to finding; an invented type is dropped unknown_type and counted\nPASS  feedback: a title over 200 chars is truncated and marked; a trace-less/malformed row is skipped and counted\nPASS  feedback: pain mapping across all three scales (finding P1/P2/P3, learning low/med/high, default 1)\nPASS  feedback: first_seen maps per kind (backlog ts, learning date, cell capped_at then claimed_at)\nPASS  feedback: cells contribute blocked/deviation presence only — trace.worker never reaches the digest bytes\nPASS  feedback: buildDigest is a byte-identical snapshot under a pinned clock (only generated_at is volatile)\nPASS  feedback: listInScope returns sorted names for an in-scope dir, [] for a file, null when absent\nPASS  mergeDigests: dogfood_repos absent → the local digest only (no foreign groups, local content untouched)\nPASS  mergeDigests: a listed dogfood repo that does not exist → warned, skipped, never thrown\nPASS  mergeDigests: a missing or corrupt foreign digest → skipped and counted, never thrown\nPASS  mergeDigests: a foreign injection title is dropped (reason injection) and every surviving foreign title is datamark-wrapped\nPASS  mergeDigests: a foreign title carrying an API key is dropped (reason secret), key absent from the merged bytes\nPASS  mergeDigests: a foreign entry carrying a field outside the allowlist has it stripped, never merged through\nPASS  mergeDigests: a symlinked foreign feedback-digest.json is rejected by realpath containment, warned, and never read\nPASS  mergeDigests: P1-1 — an injection payload in a foreign `source` (clean title) is dropped, role tags never reach the merged view\nPASS  mergeDigests: an injection payload in a foreign `layer` is dropped (reason injection), absent from the merged bytes\nPASS  mergeDigests: a secret in a foreign `source` is dropped (reason secret), the key absent from the merged bytes\nPASS  mergeDigests: a foreign `kind` outside KIND_ALIASES lands in dropped with reason unknown_type (as the local producer path does)\nPASS  mergeDigests: foreign non-string values in string fields never survive into the merged view\nPASS  mergeDigests: a foreign title over 200 chars is capped, as buildEntry caps local titles\nPASS  mergeDigests: every surviving foreign string field that can reach a prompt is datamark-wrapped, not title alone\nPASS  normalizeKind is idempotent for every alias key and every normalized kind (the regression: re-running it on an already-normalized value must not fall through to unknown_type)\nPASS  mergeDigests: a foreign digest carrying the four regressed kinds (audit, correction, approval, closed — already-normalized VALUES, exactly what a producer writes) merges with zero unknown_type drops\nPASS  mergeDigests: a foreign `kind` of {}, \"<script>\", or null is still dropped as unknown_type — the D2b re-normalization control stays intact\nPASS  round-trip: a digest produced by buildDigest and fed straight into mergeDigests loses ZERO entries (producer/consumer vocabulary symmetry — the assertion that would have caught the regression)\nPASS  feedback: ENTRY_FIELD_SPEC is the single source of truth — every field owns a validator function and ENTRY_FIELDS is exactly Object.keys(ENTRY_FIELD_SPEC) (a field added without a spec turns the suite red, not into a hole)\nPASS  feedback: source contains no `ENTRY_FIELDS = [` literal name-list assignment (the round-1/2/3 defect shape, paired with — not a replacement for — the behavioral ENTRY_FIELD_SPEC guard above)\nPASS  mergeDigests: table-driven — an injection payload AND an AWS key in ANY ENTRY_FIELD_SPEC field never reach the merged bytes (the guard that would have caught all three rounds)\nPASS  mergeDigests: the exact round-3 re-review first_seen payload (Date.parse treats the parens as a comment) is neutralized — neither the role tag nor the AWS key reaches the merged bytes, and first_seen never carries the forged value\nPASS  mergeDigests: legitimate ISO first_seen values round-trip unchanged and sort ascending (unforgeable-by-format must not reject real dates)\nPASS  normalizeTitle(datamark(t)) === normalizeTitle(t) for a plain title (the datamark asymmetry trap)\nPASS  normalizeTitle strips the datamark wrapper to FIXED POINT — a double-wrapped title also unifies (datamark double-wrap non-idempotence)\nPASS  normalizeTitle(datamark(t)) === normalizeTitle(t) for a title carrying a fence, a role tag, and control chars (plan-checker W4)\nPASS  normalizeTitle casefolds and collapses whitespace so purely-cosmetic differences never split a cluster\nPASS  normalizeTitle: distinct titles (Vietnamese vs English) never falsely unify\nPASS  clusterEntries: an empty/malformed merged view yields [] without throwing\nPASS  rankClusters: an empty cluster list yields [] without throwing\nPASS  clusterEntries: THE TRAP — a foreign wrapped title and an identical bare local title land in ONE cluster of 2\nPASS  clusterEntries: pain = max entry pain in the cluster, frequency = cluster size\nPASS  clusterEntries: corroboration is 2 when local + one synthetic foreign repo share a cluster key, 1 when disjoint\nPASS  rankClusters: rank = pain * frequency * corroboration, descending; output over a pinned digest is byte-identical across two runs\nPASS  rankClusters: deterministic tie-break — equal rank sorts by earliest first_seen ascending, then key lexicographic\nPASS  the normalized cluster key is an internal handle — clusterEntries never returns a stored title equal to the stripped key when the title differs by case/whitespace\nPASS  bee_feedback.mjs rank run directly prints valid JSON (CLI entry, like the commands_detect CLI-entry test)\nPASS  bee_state.mjs with no verb prints a Use: line listing all five verbs and exits non-zero\nPASS  bee_state.mjs set writes only the provided fields and creates state.json on a fresh repo\nPASS  bee_state.mjs set rejects an unknown phase (isKnownPhase, not the bare PHASES array) and leaves the file untouched\nPASS  bee_state.mjs set refuses to mutate a present-but-corrupt state.json (review P1-1: never clobber to defaults)\nPASS  bee_state.mjs set accepts the compounding-complete terminal alias (isKnownPhase, not PHASES)\nPASS  bee_state.mjs set preserves unrelated fields (workers, cells, last_scribing_run) byte-for-byte\nPASS  bee_state.mjs gate approves a named gate and is idempotent (same call twice = identical file)\nPASS  bee_state.mjs gate rejects an unknown gate name and a non-boolean --approved\nPASS  bee_state.mjs worker add -> update -> remove -> clear round-trips and preserves unrelated fields\nPASS  bee_state.mjs worker add rejects an unknown tier\nPASS  bee_state.mjs worker prune deletes only capped/orphan transients and keeps open-cell, active-worker (dotted ids included), subdir, and non-transient files\nPASS  bee_state.mjs worker prune --dry-run reports the exact same candidate set and deletes nothing\nPASS  bee_state.mjs worker prune rejects unknown flags (a --dryrun typo must never delete) and non-prune verbs reject --dry-run\nPASS  bee_state.mjs worker prune fails closed when state.workers is not an array (semantic corruption, valid JSON)\nPASS  bee_state.mjs worker prune over a corrupt state.json exits non-zero and deletes nothing (readStateStrict before any rm)\nPASS  bee_state.mjs worker prune with no .bee/workers dir succeeds with 0 pruned, and the unknown-action Use: line lists prune\nPASS  bee_state.mjs scribing-run stamps the exact key set from bee-scribing SKILL.md:112 including an ISO-precise at\nPASS  bee_state.mjs scribing-run accepts a single descriptive area with no comma (real-world shape)\nPASS  bee_state.mjs rejects an unknown verb with a Use: line, exit non-zero\nPASS  start-feature (lib): succeeds from idle with no leftover work, resets all four gates and writes feature/mode/phase in one call\nPASS  start-feature (lib): a prior feature carrying approved gates never lets the new feature inherit them\nPASS  bee_state.mjs start-feature requires --feature\nPASS  bee_state.mjs start-feature rejects a phase outside the closed vocabulary, zero mutations\nPASS  bee_state.mjs start-feature refuses when the current phase is not idle/terminal, zero mutations\nPASS  bee_state.mjs start-feature refuses while .bee/HANDOFF.json exists, zero mutations\nPASS  bee_state.mjs start-feature refuses while a registered worker remains, zero mutations\nPASS  bee_state.mjs start-feature refuses while an active reservation remains, zero mutations; an expired one does not block\nPASS  bee_state.mjs start-feature refuses while ANY cell anywhere is claimed, zero mutations\nPASS  bee_state.mjs start-feature refuses while the PRIOR feature has a nonterminal (open/blocked) cell, and succeeds once each is dropped via the existing drop verb (P1 repair: no auto-clear cleanup)\nPASS  bee_state.mjs start-feature: a CAPPED prior-feature cell is terminal and never blocks (only open/claimed/blocked do)\nPASS  bee_state.mjs start-feature defaults --phase to \"exploring\" and --mode to null when omitted\nPASS  bee_state.mjs start-feature rejects --dry-run (a mutating verb, same generic guard as every non-prune verb)\nPASS  bee_backlog.mjs add appends a validated row and buildDigest picks it up (never dropped as unknown_type)\nPASS  bee_backlog.mjs add accepts an already-normalized NORMALIZED_KINDS value for --type, not only a KIND_ALIASES key\nPASS  bee_backlog.mjs add rejects --type \"kind\" (the literal word) and any other unrecognized type before any write\nPASS  bee_backlog.mjs add rejects an oversize title, a bad severity, and an oversize/empty layer, leaving the file untouched\nPASS  bee_backlog.mjs add accepts an arbitrary free-string --layer with no allowlist (e.g. \"security\", already live in backlog data)\nPASS  bee_backlog.mjs counts/rank/badges verbs are unchanged by the add verb addition\nPASS  bee_backlog.mjs with no command prints a Use: line listing all four verbs and exits non-zero\nPASS  vendored source: every skills/bee-hive/templates/**/*.mjs file contains no raw C0 control byte other than tab, newline, or carriage return (a NUL byte makes grep/rg treat the file as binary and print nothing, not even a zero count)\nPASS  vendored source: every templates/*.mjs and templates/lib/*.mjs is byte-identical to its .bee/bin sibling (no standing guard existed before — a one-sided edit went green forever)\nPASS  vendored statusline: every templates/statusline/* is byte-identical to its .claude/ sibling when the repo opted in (same one-sided-edit guard as the .bee/bin sweep)\n\n169 passed, 0 failed\n",
.bee/cells/harness09-8.json:11:    "D7"
.bee/cells/harness09-8.json:20:  "action": "Per D7: grooming's Hunt Checklists gain a Fresh Session Test probe — five questions each mapped to its artifact: what is this system -> docs/specs/system-overview.md; how is it organized -> docs/specs/reading-map.md; how do I run it / verify it -> .bee/config.json commands; where are we now -> bee_status. Any unanswerable question files a backlog item naming the missing artifact. CREATION-LOG note citing docs/09 item 4.",
.bee/cells/fsh-10.json:24:    "hooks/adapter.mjs",
.bee/cells/fsh-10.json:53:      "No prose/skill-file edits (S5 owns doctrine)",
.bee/cells/fsh-10.json:75:    "verification_evidence": "{\n  \"tests_inspected\": [\n    \"skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs (fsh-9's writeCappedCellFixture/claimCellFile/writeHandoff fixtures, makeStateRepo, laneStore/laneBinding namespace imports, race_claims_child.mjs fork/barrier-file pattern)\",\n    \"hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs (runWrapper/buildFixture/copyLib harness, writeLaneFile/writeSessionFile/writeReservationsFile direct-JSON fixture style, adapterRow/findGapLine helpers)\"\n  ],\n  \"tests_added_or_changed\": [\n    \"test_lib.mjs: 4 new buildSessionPreamble rows for the handoffOutcome render contract — null (pause/kindless byte-identical, and a planned-next handoff with no outcome falls back to the identical wait block), ok:true (start-now block naming cell/lane/verify), ok:false (wait block + one reason line)\",\n    \"test_lib.mjs: 1 new two-session end-to-end race check spawning a generated temp orchestrator (never a tracked repo file) that self-forks via the exact race_claims_child.mjs barrier-file pattern: session A caps+claims+writes a planned-next handoff, session B adopts via state.mjs's adoptHandoff (the wired, handoff-aware call, not the raw claims.mjs primitive) while two concurrent thief sessions call claimCellFile on the same cell and must lose with typed CLAIMED\",\n    \"test_hook_contracts.mjs: new runHandoffSessionRows() (9 rows) registered into the default suite — source 'clear' and 'startup' (different acting session) adopt through the REAL hook child and transfer claim ownership; source 'resume'/'compact' NEVER adopt (mandatory negative rows, C11) with handoff/claim left untouched; a 'startup' whose writer_session equals the acting session also refuses (not a fresh-session boundary); pause and kindless handoffs render byte-identically regardless of session_id/source; a payload with no session_id renders byte-identically to today even with a planned-next handoff on disk and source=clear\"\n  ],\n  \"red_failure_evidence\": \"Captured via git stash (kept the two test-file edits staged, stashed only the 4 implementation files: hooks/bee-session-init.mjs, skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/inject.mjs, and their two .bee/bin vendored siblings) then reran both suites against the UNMODIFIED pre-fsh-10 hook/inject: test_lib.mjs 278 passed/2 failed (the two new handoffOutcome.ok===true/ok===false buildSessionPreamble rows — the old function signature had no handoffOutcome param and always rendered the plain wait block, so both rows failed exactly as expected); hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs 139/141 rows passing, 2 failing (session-init-handoff::clear-source-adopts-and-transfers-claim and ::startup-source-from-a-different-session-adopts — the unmodified hook never registers a session or calls adoptHandoff, so the claim stayed owned by 'sess-writer' and stdout never carried the PLANNED-NEXT ADOPTED heading). The mandatory negative rows (resume/compact never-adopt, pause/kindless byte-parity, no-session-id byte-parity) correctly passed against the unmodified hook too, since those describe behavior that was already true before this cell and stays true after it. git stash pop restored the implementation; both suites returned to green (280/0 and 141/141) confirmed by a second full run plus the two cmp vendor-parity checks.\",\n  \"verification_run\": \"node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs -> 280 passed, 0 failed; node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs -> 141 rows across 16 check group(s) (0 skipped), 0 failing target contract, ALL PASS; node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs -> PASS (failures: 0, skipped: 1); cmp -s on hooks/bee-session-init.mjs and skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/inject.mjs vs their .bee/bin siblings -> both byte-identical. Combined chain exit code 0.\",\n  \"deliberate_exceptions\": []\n}\n",

.bee/cells/worktree-isolation-2.json:4:  "title": "Hook adapter validated hop + write-guard store/root split + tool-level ambiguity deny — parity and contract tests",

.bee/cells/worktree-isolation-2.json:30:  "action": "Per D2/D4 and review repairs, run in the SHARED checkout after wt-1. Mirror wt-1's typed resolver semantics in both adapter copies without importing state.mjs: ctx.root stays canonical physical workRoot; ctx.storeRoot and ctx.worktreeResolution ordinary|linked-valid|linked-invalid are consumed only by bee-write-guard. Adapter returns typed state rather than throwing so host errors remain fail-open; every write-capable tool receives a typed deny on linked-invalid before mutation. Before logical reservation normalization, canonicalize workRoot and prove target containment: existing targets use realpath; new targets canonicalize the nearest existing ancestor then append unresolved segments; reject outside-root absolute targets, any relative result containing .., symlink escape, and Windows separator/case escape. Only contained targets become repo-relative reservation keys. Add RED-first rows for every write-tool class across traversal, absolute-main path, symlink escape, new-file ancestor, Windows separators/case, linked-invalid, owner allow, and foreign-holder deny; read-only/ordinary controls stay unchanged. Extend test_lib_mirror with a mechanically derived production-hook inventory: parse hooks/hooks.json and hooks/claude-hooks.json command targets, take the transitive closure of safe same-directory static relative imports (currently adapter), and compare that expected set with direct .bee/bin/hooks files for missing, extra, and byte drift. Source-only catalog/tests/JSON configs are excluded by derivation, never a hand list. Self-test injected missing/extra/diff. Keep declared hook pairs synchronized and regenerate release manifest before verify.",
.bee/cells/worktree-isolation-2.json:44:      "typed adapter context and containment-aware write guard in both runtime/source pairs",

.bee/cells/harness10-7.json:73:    "verification_evidence": "{\n  \"cell\": \"harness10-7\",\n  \"kind\": \"prose (documentation behavior change: bee's own PBI-layer workflow rules)\",\n  \"tests_inspected\": [\n    \"skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs (lib unit suite incl. backlog parser + preamble PBI line + optional cell pbi field, all shipped by harness10-6)\",\n    \"skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs (AGENTS block idempotency, content assertions, vendored-file copy checks)\"\n  ],\n  \"tests_added_or_changed\": \"none — this cell is prose + AGENTS block only; the runnable surfaces (bee_status pbi counts, preamble PBI line, backlog.mjs parser, optional cell pbi field) were built and tested by harness10-6. This cell references them, adds no machinery.\",\n  \"red_failure_evidence\": \"n/a — no new mechanical assertion. Regression risk was the AGENTS.block.md edit (a versioned template asserted by test_onboard_bee.mjs); baseline was green before the edit and green after, and the idempotency + content assertions ('AGENTS block mentions bee_status first step', 'user content preserved', 'third apply byte-identical') still pass, proving the one added line did not break the block contract.\",\n  \"verification_run\": {\n    \"command\": \"cell verify (grep landmarks + line-count budgets + both test suites)\",\n    \"result\": \"EXIT=0\",\n    \"test_lib\": \"59 passed, 0 failed\",\n    \"test_onboard\": \"PASS - failures: 0, skipped: 0\",\n    \"line_budgets\": \"AGENTS.block.md 91<=100; bee-scribing/SKILL.md 151<=200; bee-exploring/SKILL.md 97<=200; bee-compounding/SKILL.md 118<=200\",\n    \"landmark_greps\": \"backlog present in scribing-reference.md and AGENTS.block.md; literal token 'projection|todo list' present in routing-and-contracts.md (grep BRE treats | as literal, so the D12 invariant is named with that exact token)\"\n  },\n  \"single_owner_check\": \"backlog table schema + status enum + merge rules exist only in skills/bee-scribing/references/scribing-reference.md (Product Backlog section). Other skills point to it, none duplicate the table (D6).\",\n  \"flip_triggers_placed\": {\n    \"D11a in-flight\": \"skills/bee-exploring/SKILL.md Scope step\",\n    \"D11b done\": \"skills/bee-scribing/SKILL.md sync (section 4 close-loop) + scribing-reference merge rules; compounding fallback in skills/bee-compounding/SKILL.md section 6\"\n  },\n  \"prohibitions_honored\": [\n    \"D7: no hook/helper enforcement of PBI transitions — every flip/audit line states 'prose-ruled, never hook-enforced'\",\n    \"no new gates, no gate-wording changes\",\n    \"no mechanical code changes (prose + AGENTS block line only)\",\n    \"no second location for the backlog table schema\"\n  ]\n}\n",

unapproved own lane is NEVER selected, even when its cell is the only ready one anywhere — typed NO_APPROVED_WORK (D2 authority boundary)\nPASS  claimNextCell: own lane with no ready cells falls through to another execution-approved lane\nPASS  claimNextCell: cross-lane ordering — when own pipeline is empty/unbound, the pool of other approved lanes is ordered by backlog rank first, then lane created_at (D2)\nPASS  claimNextCell: a cell whose files intersect ANOTHER session's active hold is skipped; the acting session's own hold on the same files never excludes it (D3)\nPASS  claimNextCell: the acting session's OWN active hold on a cell's files never excludes it\nPASS  claimCellCrossSession: a claimCell THROW after the claim file was created releases the claim file — no orphan (W4 unwind pin)\nPASS  claimNextCell: a repo with no lanes and no session record at all (pure default pipeline) still claims cleanly — D4 zero-lane shape\nPASS  claimNextCell: NO_APPROVED_WORK write\nPASS  bee.mjs backlog add rejects an oversize title, a bad severity, and an oversize/empty layer, leaving the file untouched\nPASS  bee.mjs backlog add accepts an arbitrary free-string --layer with no allowlist (e.g. \"security\", already live in backlog data)\nPASS  bee.mjs backlog counts/rank/badges verbs are unchanged by the add verb addition\nPASS  bee.mjs backlog with no command prints a Use: line listing all four verbs and exits non-zero\nPASS  createReview: session roundtrip carries every SPEC §8 field, and show/readReview round-trips it\nPASS  createReview: A10 fails closed — a behavior_change cell with no verification_evidence refuses create and writes NO session file\nPASS  createReview: A6 auto-excludes an open/claimed included cell with reason \"in progress\", never silently reviewed-in\nPASS  createReview: a pre-declared \"excluded\" entry in the scope input is preserved alongside auto-exclusions\nPASS  createReview: refuses an already-existing session id with non-zero-equivalent throw and leaves the file byte-unchanged (id non-reuse, §8)\nPASS  createReview: rejects missing required scope fields and an empty \"included\" array before any write\nPASS  recordOnReview: refuses any payload touching baseline/head/included/excluded — exits via throw, file byte-unchanged (R5 immutability)\nPASS  recordOnReview: manifest/preflight/decision SET the field; finding/uat APPEND one entry per call\nPASS  recordOnReview: rejects an unknown kind before touching the file\nreviews: skipping corrupt session file rev-corrupt.json (list stays fail-open)\nPASS  reviews: strict read fails loud on a corrupt session (write verbs fail closed) — readReview/list stay fail-open\nPASS  recordOnReview: refuses a session id that does not exist (nothing to mutate)\nPASS  candidate ledger: addCandidate requires --mode from the closing feature's lane, appends exactly one JSONL line, never rewrites prior lines\nPASS  candidate ledger: a corrupt line is skipped on read (fail-open), good lines still returned\nPASS  bee.mjs reviews create/show/list/record/candidate round-trip through the CLI, --file and --stdin both work\nPASS  bee.mjs reviews create exits non-zero and writes nothing when the A10 preflight fails\nPASS  bee.mjs reviews candidate add requires --mode and rejects an unrecognized mode, leaving the ledger untouched\nPASS  bee.mjs reviews with no command prints a Use: line listing all verbs and exits non-zero\nPASS  deriveCandidateStatus: a legacy candidate with no covering session derives \"unreviewed\" — no fake session records fabricated (SPEC §11.3)\nPASS  deriveCandidateStatus: a non-approved (pending) session whose scope includes the candidate's feature derives \"in review\"\nPASS  deriveCandidateStatus: a blocked (P1-pending) session still derives \"in review\", never \"reviewed\" (R8 — P1 blocks approval)\nPASS  deriveCandidateStatus: an approved session covers the candidate's exact head as \"reviewed\"; one extra commit after that head flips the SAME candidate to \"review stale\" while the session file stays byte-unchanged (A8)\nPASS  deriveCandidateStatus: an unresolvable candidate head (unknown sha, simulating rebase/amend) with a covering approved session degrades to \"review stale\" with a \"range unresolvable\" note, never throws (plan open question 1)\nPASS  deriveCandidateStatus: git binary unavailable (PATH stripped) never throws — a covering session degrades to \"review stale\"/\"range unresolvable\", read path stays usable\nPASS  deriveCandidateStatus: a candidate whose head postdates the covering approved session's frozen head (new work, same feature, no new session) derives \"unreviewed\" — not a stale re-labelling of unrelated new work\nPASS  deriveCandidateStatus: CANDIDATE_STATUSES exports exactly the four R10 labels\nPASS  bee.mjs reviews status: --json renders verified + four-label counts and per-candidate coverage, \"reviewed (covered by <id>)\" answers A7\nPASS  bee.mjs reviews status: --feature filters the candidate set, and a repo with zero candidates still renders all-zero counts at exit 0\nPASS  bee.mjs status --json review block distinguishes all four candidate statuses (unreviewed/in_review/reviewed/stale), lists open sessions, and flags a high-risk unreviewed candidate (R7/R10)\nPASS  bee.mjs status: a compounding-complete state with gate \"review\" pending produces NO staleness warning (R3 — the retired Gate-4-pending warning never fires); the §9 completion line renders in text instead, naming the unreviewed count\nPASS  bee.mjs status: the §9 completion line only renders in a post-execution phase — it stays silent mid-swarm even with unreviewed candidates present, and stays silent post-execution with zero candidates\nPASS  bee.mjs status: the unknown-phase warning (decision 0004) still fires unchanged after the review-block wiring\nPASS  bee.mjs status: recommended_next after compounding-complete with unreviewed candidates reports the candidate count and never names \"Invoke bee-reviewing\" as the automatic next step (§11.5), even overriding a stale state.next_action that did\nPASS  bee.mjs status: a high-risk unreviewed candidate renders the prominent R7 warning line, and a repo with only non-high-risk candidates renders no such line\nPASS  bee.mjs status: a standard-mode candidate never triggers the R7 high-risk warning line, even when unreviewed\nPASS  bee.mjs status: a corrupt .bee/reviews entry and an unreadable candidates ledger degrade the review block but leave bee_status exiting 0 (fail-open read path, never a hard dependency)\nPASS  vendored source: every skills/bee-hive/templates/**/*.mjs file contains no raw C0 control byte other than tab, newline, or carriage return (a NUL byte makes grep/rg treat the file as binary and print nothing, not even a zero count)\nPASS  vendored source: every templates/*.mjs and templates/lib/*.mjs is byte-identical to its .bee/bin sibling (no standing guard existed before — a one-sided edit went green forever)\nPASS  vendored statusline: every templates/statusline/* is byte-identical to its .claude/ sibling when the repo opted in (same one-sided-edit guard as the .bee/bin sweep)\nPASS  census: retired auto-review-trigger phrasing is absent from every live prose surface (skills SKILL.md + references, AGENTS.md + AGENTS.block.md template, living docs/*.md + docs/specs/*.md) — docs/history and docs/decisions archaeology excluded (critical patterns 20260711/20260712)\nPASS  census: the on-demand review contract carries its required anchors — AGENTS.block.md keeps the on-request bee-reviewing side entry, bee-compounding keeps the review-candidate close step\nPASS  census: the Delegation contract (fan-out) lives in the always-loaded doctrine layer — AGENTS.block.md + root AGENTS.md carry the rubric, not just the bee-hive reference\nPASS  census: the two-kind handoff rule (with its transport) and the multi-session etiquette rule live in the always-loaded doctrine layer — AGENTS.block.md + root AGENTS.md carry both, not just the runtime lib (fresh-session-handoff S5, D1/D3)\nPASS  no shipped SKILL.md or reference instructs a --phase value outside KNOWN_PHASES (chain-integrity D6)\nPASS  classifySource: source_checkout = plugin.json + .git at the package root\nPASS  classifySource: project_projection = launcher under .agents/skills or .claude/skills\nPASS  classifySource: plugin_package = plugin.json but NO .git; never a global authority (SRC-03)\nPASS  classifySource: legacy_global = source root is ~/.claude/skills by realpath (checked before projection)\nPASS  classifySource: unknown (fail-closed) — no manifest, and an unparseable manifest too (sentinel TEST-01)\nPASS  classifySource: never throws on odd input — returns unknown\nPASS  classifySource: pure — classifying mutates nothing\nPASS  computeSchedule: chain A<-B<-C schedules across three waves in dep order\nPASS  computeSchedule: diamond A->{B,C}->D packs B and C into one wave\nPASS  computeSchedule: overlap-serialize — trailing-* glob vs a concrete path defers to the next wave (D2/D3)\nPASS  computeSchedule: mid-path glob is a literal (not a glob engine) — no overlap, same wave\nPASS  detectCycles: chain has no cycle; self-dep a->a is its own single-member cycle\nPASS  detectCycles: two-cycle A<->B is reported regardless of status (structural, spans on-disk + in-batch)\nPASS  computeSchedule: a dependency cycle never crashes — members excluded from every wave, reported in diagnostics.cycles\nPASS  computeSchedule: dep on a missing/blocked/dropped cell is unsatisfiable — excluded from waves, never a crash\nPASS  computeSchedule: unsatisfiable exclusion propagates transitively without its own diagnostics row\nPASS  computeSchedule: empty files overlaps nothing — schedules in the earliest ready wave and is flagged in diagnostics\nPASS  computeSchedule: a dep on a capped cell is satisfied — no schedule edge, dependent runs in the first wave\nPASS  computeSchedule: deterministic — same input twice, and input order does not change the result\nPASS  computeSchedule / detectCycles: empty input yields empty, well-shaped output\n\n321 passed, 0 failed\nPASS test_lib_mirror: internal self-test — checker correctly flags injected byte-diff, missing file, and extra file\nPASS test_lib_mirror: templates/lib and .bee/bin/lib are byte-identical (17 files)\n",
.bee/cells/codex-hook-state-parity-3.json:17:    "D7",
.bee/cells/codex-hook-state-parity-3.json:20:    "D10",
.bee/cells/codex-hook-state-parity-3.json:66:  "action": "Implement exclusive plugin-first and repo-copy distribution across both installers (D9-D14). Add one shared Node helper used by Bash/PowerShell to discover the installed bee package, prove it enabled, compare its complete canonical skills plus runtime hook payload against the release inventory, maintain an exact installer ownership ledger for user roots, preflight all cleanup targets, and apply only after every proof passes. Plugin-first removes only plain owned bee-* project skill directories and catalog-recognized bee hook entries from Claude/Codex project configuration while preserving every user entry/container; symlink, alias, file, unknown root, corrupt ledger, or inventory mismatch refuses the whole cleanup with zero mutation (D10, D11, D14). Repo-copy first disables/uninstalls the plugin and proves it inactive before onboarding vendors project skills/hooks; failure leaves the repo untouched (D12). Keep runtime modes explicit and idempotent. Expand release inventory to include canonical plugin skills, both hook payloads, manifests, and the shared distribution machinery; add executable fixture tests for dry-run/apply/repeat, plugin proof failure, user-content survival, hook cleanup, reverse transition, ledger failure, Bash/PowerShell parity, cachebuster/reinstall fixture, and source-checkout fallback distinction (D13). Add the new suite to the configured verify manifest without weakening any existing suite. Synchronize current docs/specs and prove the corrected worktree state/review routing remains unchanged. Actual public publication remains outside this cell.",

Complete 613-line output is stored in trace.verify_output.\",\n  \"deliberate_exceptions\": [\n    \"No protected .codex/.agents projections, commit, or independent review were performed, per dispatch.\"\n  ]\n}\n",

.bee/cells/codex-parity-6c.json:29:    "hooks/adapter.mjs",
.bee/cells/codex-parity-6c.json:38:  "action": "Make the source-repo Codex PreToolUse (write-guard) transport fail CLOSED and stop resolving its root through git, using an OUTERMOST bee-marker walk and a single-sourced guard root (design B, per plan-repo-fallback-split.md; Gate-2 approved 2026-07-12). MATCHER HARDENING IS NOT IN THIS CELL — it moved to codex-parity-6d. Root cause (validation-codex-parity-6c.md): the transport resolved root via `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` while the node guard resolves via a nearest-ancestor `.bee/onboarding.json` walk (hooks/adapter.mjs:207 findRepoRoot) — two disagreeing root definitions, opening P1-a (bare repo -> empty root -> fail-open ALLOW) and P1-b/spoof (a both-markers root planted under an allowlisted-writable prefix .spikes//docs//plans/ shadows the real root under nearest-ancestor). (1) In hooks/catalog.mjs, replace git resolution for ALL nine repo-target commands with a POSIX ancestor walk that returns the OUTERMOST (highest) directory carrying BOTH `.bee/onboarding.json` AND `hooks/<script>` — proven to defeat the both-markers spoof in .spikes/codex-runtime-parity/marker-walk-outermost.sh (nearest=ALLOW bypass, outermost=DENY). The walk must terminate correctly at filesystem root (`${d%/*}`, handle `/`). Thread `event` into commandFor -> repoCommand; the plugin branch of commandFor must never read event so the plugin-target Codex and Claude projections stay byte-identical (proven byte-safe). (2) The PreToolUse command passes the resolved root to the guard explicitly: `exec node \"$r\"/hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs --source=repo --repo-root=\"$r\"`. In hooks/adapter.mjs + hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs, accept `--repo-root=<path>`: when present AND it contains the `.bee/onboarding.json` marker, use it as the authoritative root instead of findRepoRoot(cwd); when absent (the plugin route), fall back to the current walk unchanged so the plugin path stays byte- and behavior-identical. This single-sources the root and removes the two-root disagreement rather than relying on toRelPath's accidental escape-deny (bee-write-guard.mjs:175-194). (2b) GUARD-SIDE CAUSAL CLOSE of the above-root spoof (round-3, per decision ec629bfe + Gate-2 harden-guard choice; spike .spikes/codex-runtime-parity/guard-deny-marker-forge.mjs and aboveroot-plant.sh): the outermost walk alone only moves the spoof upward — an untrusted agent can plant BOTH markers ABOVE the real root because the guard currently escape-ALLOWS out-of-repo Write/Edit (rel=null -> no deny, bee-write-guard.mjs:211-214), and outermost then selects the attacker root and execs the attacker's guard script. Close it at the source: in hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs, BEFORE the existing in-repo checks and on EVERY write branch (Edit/Write, Bash-extracted targets, apply_patch targets), DENY (exit 2, corrective) any write whose resolved absolute target is a marker-forging path — basename `onboarding.json` directly under a `.bee/` dir, or basename `bee-write-guard.mjs` directly under a `hooks/` dir — that resolves OUTSIDE the resolved real root. CRITICAL (round-3 red-team, spike redteam-symlink-forge.mjs): containment MUST be REALPATH-anchored, not lexical. A directory symlink planted under an allowlisted prefix (e.g. `ln -s ../.. .spikes/evil`, itself ungoverned + allowlisted) makes a write LEXICALLY in-repo while it PHYSICALLY lands above the real root — lexical path.relative misses it and the marker plants succeed. Resolve BOTH the real root and the target's deepest existing ancestor through fs.realpathSync before the path.relative escape comparison (see the red-team's realParent/escapesRoot fix), and apply the same realpath anchoring to toRelPath so any GOVERNED write whose PHYSICAL destination escapes root is treated as an escape. Legit in-repo and /tmp writes stay PASS-THROUGH (spike-proven, fixedDecision column). Add the symlink plant as a REQUIRED regression arm. Document the residual HONESTLY and wider than before: (i) UNGOVERNED write paths on Codex (unified_exec / native writes — spec Open Gaps), and (ii) GOVERNED Bash primitives that extractBashTargets does not model (dd/install/python -c open/rsync — a pre-existing extractBashTargets gap, red-team-confirmed) can still plant a marker with no symlink. Neither is closed by this cell; both are the D2 'hooks are not a complete security boundary' limit, recorded as Open Gaps, NOT claimed closed. Do NOT represent the marker-forge deny as absolute. (3) PreToolUse fails CLOSED: if no outermost bee root resolves, emit one corrective diagnostic on stderr and exit 2. Pin that literal as an exported constant (e.g. REPO_TRANSPORT_DENIED_DIAGNOSTIC). The eight advisory commands (SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PostToolUse, SubagentStop x2, PreCompact, Stop x2) run the same outermost walk but on failure emit the pinned visible diagnostic and exit 0 (fail OPEN) — never exit 2 (a block/turn-control verdict on Stop/SubagentStop, violating B2/R4) and never a bare MODULE_NOT_FOUND crash (violating R2 'visible, never silent' and B1). (4) The advisory diagnostic REPO_TRANSPORT_UNAVAILABLE_DIAGNOSTIC currently says 'no git root' which is now false — reword it to name a missing bee root and update 6b's mechanical assertion + the stale catalog comments to match. (5) Regenerate .codex/hooks.json byte-for-byte from renderProjectionText('codex',{target:'repo'}). (6) hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs: (a) REWORK route arms 3 and 4 — off-git resolution inverts them: arm 4 (git off PATH) must now assert resolve-and-run proving git-independence (PreToolUse exit 2, advisories exit 0), and arm 3 (non-git cwd, no bee root above) must assert PreToolUse fail-CLOSED exit 2 + corrective while advisories fail-open exit 0; do NOT keep uniform expectTransportFailOpen on arms 3/4. (b) ADD a below-root spoof route arm: a both-markers root planted under `.spikes/` with cwd nested inside it and an ABSOLUTE apply_patch/Edit to the real .bee/state.json -> assert exit 2 (outermost walk + single-sourced root defeats it; the spoof guard never runs). (b2) ADD an above-root plant arm: from a real bee fixture root, attempt a Write/Edit/Bash/apply_patch that CREATES a marker file (`<above>/.bee/onboarding.json` or `<above>/hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs`) OUTSIDE the fixture root -> assert the guard DENIES exit 2 (the plant is blocked at the source), and a control legit out-of-repo write to a non-marker path (e.g. an absolute /tmp scratch file) still exits 0. (c) ADD an onboarding-marker arm: a foreign root that HAS hooks/ but NO `.bee/onboarding.json` must be skipped (proves the onboarding.json half is load-bearing, not just hooks/). (d) split the static codex-repo-target-transport catalog row so 8 advisory commands assert visible-fail-open and the 1 PreToolUse asserts corrective + exit 2; add codex-repo-target-transport AND a new codex-repo-target-pretooluse-failclosed row to REQUIRED_CATALOG_ROW_IDS; add the new route-arm ids to routeRequiredRowIds. Note two couplings the split touches (FEAS): `launchesSourceWrappers` is anchored on `--source=repo$` (test_hook_contracts.mjs:681-683) and will fail on the new `--source=repo --repo-root=\"$r\"` PreToolUse command, and `visibleFailOpen` requires exit 0 in EVERY command (:684-688) and will fail on the fail-closed PreToolUse command — loosen the `--source=repo$` anchor and scope the exit-0 requirement to the 8 advisory commands, not just the `$r` var-name regex. When validating --repo-root in the guard (SEC), require BOTH markers present at the passed root (matching the walk), not just `.bee/onboarding.json`. (7) Finalize docs/specs/hook-runtime.md R2 and B6 (a deny-capable checkpoint fails CLOSED when its transport cannot start; 'unknown' is not 'safe' for a deny hook) and remove the 'under revision pending 6c' qualifier from the scribing Open Gap. Preserve the plugin-target Codex projection and the Claude projection byte-for-byte.",

.bee/cells/codex-harness-hardening-4.json:6:  "action": "Create docs/history/codex-harness-hardening/snapshots/capability-snapshot.json capturing host capability surface (SPEC §3, §5.2, E-12, D-05/D-06). Record codex 0.144.4; Codex tool surface (outer functions.exec, nested tools.exec_command, tools.apply_patch; collaboration spawn_agent{task_name,message,fork_turns} + followup_task; no model selector); Claude profile (Edit/Write/MultiEdit/Bash/Read/Glob/Grep, Agent/Task). Each field carries provenance documented|live_probed; mark nested-event interception and fresh-task discovery provenance=pending_live_probe (L6/L7 deferred per D-05).",
.bee/cells/codex-harness-hardening-4.json:18:  "verify": "node -e \"const s=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('docs/history/codex-harness-hardening/snapshots/capability-snapshot.json','utf8')); if(!s.codex||!s.claude) throw new Error('missing host'); if(s.codex.version!=='0.144.4') throw new Error('codex version'); const c=JSON.stringify(s.codex); if(!(c.includes('functions.exec')&&c.includes('exec_command')&&c.includes('apply_patch'))) throw new Error('codex tool surface'); if(!(c.includes('spawn_agent')&&c.includes('followup_task'))) throw new Error('collaboration'); if(!JSON.stringify(s.claude).match(/Edit|Write|Bash/)) throw new Error('claude profile'); if(!JSON.stringify(s).includes('pending_live_probe')) throw new Error('must mark deferred live probes'); console.log('SNAPSHOT-OK');\"",
.bee/cells/codex-harness-hardening-4.json:22:      "records codex 0.144.4, outer functions.exec + nested exec_command/apply_patch, collaboration spawn_agent{task_name,message,fork_turns}+followup_task",
.bee/cells/codex-harness-hardening-4.json:51:    "verify_command": "node -e \"const s=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('docs/history/codex-harness-hardening/snapshots/capability-snapshot.json','utf8')); if(!s.codex||!s.claude) throw new Error('missing host'); if(s.codex.version!=='0.144.4') throw new Error('codex version'); const c=JSON.stringify(s.codex); if(!(c.includes('functions.exec')&&c.includes('exec_command')&&c.includes('apply_patch'))) throw new Error('codex tool surface'); if(!(c.includes('spawn_agent')&&c.includes('followup_task'))) throw new Error('collaboration'); if(!JSON.stringify(s.claude).match(/Edit|Write|Bash/)) throw new Error('claude profile'); if(!JSON.stringify(s).includes('pending_live_probe')) throw new Error('must mark deferred live probes'); console.log('SNAPSHOT-OK');\"",

excludes cells with uncapped deps\nPASS  claimCell refuses a cell with uncapped deps even after gate approval\nPASS  claimCell claims an open, dep-free cell\nPASS  capCell refuses without a passing verify result\nPASS  capCell refuses when verify was recorded as failed\nPASS  capCell refuses behavior_change without verification_evidence\nPASS  capCell caps with passing verify + evidence, and unlocks dependents\nPASS  capCell on a high-risk cell requires files_changed and outcome\nPASS  capCell refuses a small cell whose verify has no output and no evidence (decision 0004)\nPASS  capCell refuses a small cell with proof but empty files_changed (decision 0004)\nPASS  tiny lane still caps on a passing verify alone (lanes scale strictness)\nPASS  capCell honors the cell-declared behavior_change when the flag is omitted (grooming fix)\nPASS  isKnownPhase accepts the enum + terminal alias and rejects drift\nPASS  blockCell records the reason\nPASS  reserve succeeds, then conflicts for another agent on the same path\nPASS  same agent does not conflict with itself; directory prefix overlaps\nPASS  release frees the path for other agents\nPASS  sweepExpired releases TTL-expired reservations\nPASS  checkWrite blocks source writes while idle (intake gate); config can disable it\nPASS  checkWrite blocks source writes in a gated phase without execution approval\nPASS  checkWrite blocks unreserved conflicting writes during swarming\nPASS  checkRead denies secrets with a privacy marker, and generated dirs\nPASS  extractBashTargets flags sed -i and redirection targets\nPASS  logDecision rejects secrets and instruction-like content\nPASS  supersede removes the old decision from the active set\nPASS  datamark neutralizes fences and role tags\nPASS  buildPromptReminder returns text + stable hash; dedup honors the hash\nPASS  buildSessionPreamble mentions phase and gates\nPASS  readConfig returns empty commands when config.json absent\nPASS  buildSessionPreamble omits commands section when none recorded\nPASS  readConfig keeps only known non-empty string commands\nPASS  buildSessionPreamble shows commands and baseline gate when verify recorded\nPASS  cap-refusal message carries a FIX (the verify command to run)\nPASS  gate-block reason carries a FIX (route to approval)\nPASS  reservation-conflict reason carries a FIX (reserve or line (D5+D10)\nPASS  detectCommands returns [] on a repo with no manifests\nPASS  detectCommands maps package.json scripts to invocable npm commands\nPASS  detectCommands maps Makefile targets, never recipe bodies\nPASS  detectCommands dedups: package.json beats Makefile on the same key\nPASS  detectCommands proposes ecosystem conventions only without an explicit match\nPASS  commands_detect.mjs run directly prints JSON candidates (CLI entry)\nPASS  readBacklogCounts returns null when docs/backlog.md is absent\nPASS  readBacklogCounts counts a well-formed backlog by Status column\nPASS  readBacklogCounts tolerates extra columns, reordering, and bold markup\nPASS  readBacklogCounts skips malformed and unknown-status rows without throwing\nPASS  readBacklogCounts counts duplicate IDs honestly (row-by-row, dedup is grooming prose)\nPASS  BACKLOG_STATUSES is the locked D6 enum and matches its source literal (drift guard)\nPASS  addCell persists an optional pbi string and cap ignores it (no validation coupling)\nPASS  addCell rejects a non-string pbi but accepts a missing/stale one\nPASS  scribingDebt tracks behavior_change caps against the last scribing run\nPASS  modelForTier resolves runtime-keyed tiers: defaults, overrides, fallbacks\nPASS  cell tier: validation, tierMix, and the ceiling scarcity warning\nPASS  advisorModel: off by default, resolves advisor.model only at configured points\nPASS  resolveTier types every tier shape: inherit, model, budget, cli\nPASS  review slot: opus default, generation fallback, cli allowed, effort knob\nPASS  readConfig normalizes dogfood_repos: string + object shapes → {path,label}, junk ignored, dead repo warned+skipped, absent → []\nPASS  frozenJudgeHits flags judge files changed outside the declared scope\nPASS  rankBacklog groups rows in-flight → proposed → done, stable within groups\nPASS  backlog badges render counts and refresh idempotently in README markers\nPASS  capture queue: add, pending, flush, and surfacing contracts\nPASS  feedback: SCHEMA_VERSION, ENTRY_FIELDS, DROP_REASONS pinned to their source literals (drift guard)\nPASS  feedback: source contains no bare fs.<read> call and no aliased node:fs read import (read-scope drift guard)\nPASS  feedback: resolveInScope returns a real absolute path, null when absent, and throws on every escape\nPASS  feedback: a symlinked cell escaping the repo is rejected by realpath containment, warned, and never read\nPASS  feedback: empty repo yields a valid zero-count snapshot without throwing (absent sources skipped + counted)\nPASS  feedback: the allowlist carries no free text — friction detail naming readBacklogCounts/COMMAND_KEYS never reaches the digest\nPASS  feedback: a secret in a title is dropped as a security event (scan runs BEFORE truncation), key absent from bytes\nPASS  feedback: an injection payload in a title is dropped as injection; dropped shape carries the category only\nPASS  feedback: kind vocabulary — review-finding maps to finding; an invented type is dropped unknown_type and counted\nPASS  feedback: a title over 200 chars is truncated and marked; a trace-less/malformed row is skipped and counted\nPASS  feedback: pain mapping across all three scales (finding P1/P2/P3, learning low/med/high, default 1)\nPASS  feedback: first_seen maps per kind (backlog ts, learning date, cell capped_at then claimed_at)\nPASS  feedback: cells contribute blocked/deviation presence only — trace.worker never reaches the digest bytes\nPASS  feedback: buildDigest is a byte-identical snapshot under a pinned clock (only generated_at is volatile)\nPASS  feedback: listInScope returns sorted names for an in-scope dir, [] for a file, null when absent\nPASS  mergeDigests: dogfood_repos absent → the local digest only (no foreign groups, local content untouched)\nPASS  mergeDigests: a listed dogfood repo that does not exist → warned, skipped, never thrown\nPASS  mergeDigests: a missing or corrupt foreign digest → skipped and counted, never thrown\nPASS  mergeDigests: a foreign injection title is dropped (reason injection) and every surviving foreign title is datamark-wrapped\nPASS  mergeDigests: a foreign title carrying an API key is dropped (reason secret), key absent from the merged bytes\nPASS  mergeDigests: a foreign entry carrying a field outside the allowlist has it stripped, never merged through\nPASS  mergeDigests: a symlinked foreign feedback-digest.json is rejected by realpath containment, warned, and never read\nPASS  mergeDigests: P1-1 — an injection payload in a foreign `source` (clean title) is dropped, role tags never reach the merged view\nPASS  mergeDigests: an injection payload in a foreign `layer` is dropped (reason injection), absent from the merged bytes\nPASS  mergeDigests: a secret in a foreign `source` is dropped (reason secret), the key absent from the merged bytes\nPASS  mergeDigests: a foreign `kind` outside KIND_ALIASES lands in dropped with reason unknown_type (as the local producer path does)\nPASS  mergeDigests: foreign non-string values in string fields never survive into the merged view\nPASS  mergeDigests: a foreign title over 200 chars is capped, as buildEntry caps local titles\nPASS  mergeDigests: every surviving foreign string field that can reach a prompt is datamark-wrapped, not title alone\nPASS  normalizeKind is idempotent for every alias key and every normalized kind (the regression: re-running it on an already-normalized value must not fall through to unknown_type)\nPASS  mergeDigests: a foreign digest carrying the four regressed kinds (audit, correction, approval, closed — already-normalized VALUES, exactly what a producer writes) merges with zero unknown_type drops\nPASS  mergeDigests: a foreign `kind` of {}, \"<script>\", or null is still dropped as unknown_type — the D2b re-normalization control stays intact\nPASS  round-trip: a digest produced by buildDigest and fed straight into mergeDigests loses ZERO entries (producer/consumer vocabulary symmetry — the assertion that would have caught the regression)\nPASS  feedback: ENTRY_FIELD_SPEC is the single source of truth — every field owns a validator function and ENTRY_FIELDS is exactly Object.keys(ENTRY_FIELD_SPEC) (a field added without a spec turns the suite red, not into a hole)\nPASS  feedback: source contains no `ENTRY_FIELDS = [` literal name-list assignment (the round-1/2/3 defect shape, paired with — not a replacement for — the behavioral ENTRY_FIELD_SPEC guard above)\nPASS  mergeDigests: table-driven — an injection payload AND an AWS key in ANY ENTRY_FIELD_SPEC field never reach the merged bytes (the guard that would have caught all three rounds)\nPASS  mergeDigests: the exact round-3 re-review first_seen payload (Date.parse treats the parens as a comment) is neutralized — neither the role tag nor the AWS key reaches the merged bytes, and first_seen never carries the forged value\nPASS  mergeDigests: legitimate ISO first_seen values round-trip unchanged and sort ascending (unforgeable-by-format must not reject real dates)\nPASS  normalizeTitle(datamark(t)) === normalizeTitle(t) for a plain title (the datamark asymmetry trap)\nPASS  normalizeTitle strips the datamark wrapper to FIXED POINT — a double-wrapped title also unifies (datamark double-wrap non-idempotence)\nPASS  normalizeTitle(datamark(t)) === normalizeTitle(t) for a title carrying a fence, a role tag, and control chars (plan-checker W4)\nPASS  normalizeTitle casefolds and collapses whitespace so purely-cosmetic differences never split a cluster\nPASS  normalizeTitle: distinct titles (Vietnamese vs English) never falsely unify\nPASS  clusterEntries: an empty/malformed merged view yields [] without throwing\nPASS  rankClusters: an empty cluster list yields [] without throwing\nPASS  clusterEntries: THE TRAP — a foreign wrapped title and an identical bare local title land in ONE cluster of 2\nPASS  clusterEntries: pain = max entry pain in the cluster, frequency = cluster size\nPASS  clusterEntries: corroboration is 2 when local + one synthetic foreign repo share a cluster No hard-gate flag (no auth/data-loss/security/provider-secret surface).     23	| S3 | Plugin hooks bundling + XOR rule; approval_policy out of distributed default + profiles | D6 D7 | S2 |

    25	| S5 | Adapter split for 5 runtime-sensitive skills (render at onboarding sync) | D9 | S2 (informs adapter content), regression net first |

Matrix verdicts (decision logged): DEFER D6 (plugin hooks removed/false on 0.144.4) and D8 (custom agents not discovered; P25 stays deferred); PROCEED D7, D10, S6 doctor; observed bonus — `update_plan` reaches PostToolUse and `spawn_agent` fires PreToolUse with `tool_input.agent_type`.
    30	
    31	**Current slice (slice 2 = S3+S4 revised):**
    32	1. **cnr2-6 (D7, docs):** approval_policy is NOT distributed (verified: repo-local only) — document the Codex-permission-vs-gate-bypass distinction + `bee-safe`/`bee-autopilot` profiles in INSTALL.md and docs/06-runtime-integration.md.

    42	3. ~~cnr2-3 (S1/D5)~~ — dropped at validation: premise disproved (manifests are intentional catalog projections; drift-check already pins them — see CONTEXT.md D5 correction).

    48	- **Version skew:** superset matcher must be valid on both old and new Codex tool names (that is why superset, never swap — D4).

    50	- **Fail-open logging:** hook edits must keep `logs/hooks.jsonl` fail-open behavior (covered by `test_hook_contracts.mjs`).
    51	- **Distribution:** template-vs-rendered drift is the standing failure mode (generator drift check from 1.5.0) — every rendered-file edit lands at its template source.

/bin/bash -lc "nl -ba docs/history/codex-native-runtime-v2/CONTEXT.md | sed -n '1,75p'" in /home/thanhsmind/projects/goglbe/beegog
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# CONTEXT — codex-native-runtime-v2
     2	
     3	**Goal:** bee on Codex delivers the same operating experience as on Claude Code — same invariants, runtime-native orchestration — per the external review `docs/REFs/be-codex.md`, verified against repo reality on 2026-07-18 (all 8 of its structural claims confirmed; see conversation review + gather digests).
     4	
     5	**Mode:** high-risk (5 flags: external systems, public contracts, cross-platform, existing covered behavior, multi-domain). No hard-gate flag; gate_bypass=total is active.
     6	
     7	## Verified problem statement
     8	
     9	1. Three active docs still claim "Codex has no lifecycle hooks" (`INSTALL.md:120`, `docs/06-runtime-integration.md:52`, `README.md:434`) while `.codex/hooks.json` ships 7 events — bee's own P24 feature (`docs/history/codex-runtime-parity/CONTEXT.md:88-90`) already flagged this drift; the fix never landed.
    10	2. No hook-trust verification exists for Codex (no `/hooks` step; file presence is treated as capability).
    11	3. `.codex/hooks.json` state-sync matcher is Claude-named (`TaskCreate|TaskUpdate|TodoWrite`); no `update_plan`; no PreToolUse guard for `Agent` (no model-guard equivalent on Codex).
    12	4. `.codex-plugin/plugin.json` bundles skills only — no `hooks` key.
    13	5. `.codex/config.toml` ships `approval_policy = "never"` as the only content; no `[agents]`, no `.codex/agents/` roles (P25 deferred pending "reliable role selection").
    14	6. Worker invariants live only in `bee-executing/SKILL.md` prose (user-tier on Codex); nothing at developer priority.
    15	7. The 5 runtime-sensitive skills ship byte-identical to both runtimes (verified same SHA) with interleaved Claude/Codex branches; advisor transport for model-shaped advisors falls back to `claude -p` (`bee-executing/SKILL.md:90-93`) even on Codex.
    16	8. ~~NEW: Claude-side split-brain~~ — DISPROVED at validation: the two manifests are intentional per-runtime catalog projections (see D5 correction below).
    17	
    18	## Locked decisions
    19	
    20	- **D1 — One Bee Core, no skill fork.** Shared workflow semantics stay single-source; only runtime-sensitive surfaces get adapters. (Review doc final table; aligns decision 565e68d0 lineage.)
    21	- **D2 — Capability-gated build.** Every claimed new Codex capability (`.codex/agents/*.toml` discovery, `developer_instructions`, plugin `hooks` key, PreToolUse `Agent`-shaped matcher, `update_plan` tool name, SubagentStart-equivalent) must be **observed on the installed Codex CLI** by a read-only spike before any slice builds on it. A capability not observed ⇒ that slice degrades to the current mechanism and logs an explicit asymmetry decision (AO11 pattern). File-presence is never capability (`hooks_file_present ≠ hooks_discovered ≠ hooks_trusted_and_observed`).
    22	- **D3 — Truth cleanup lands first.** Remove/replace every active-doc "Codex has no (lifecycle) hooks" claim (INSTALL.md, README.md, docs/06-runtime-integration.md; config-reference/decision docs keep historical wording with a dated correction note only where they are active guidance). INSTALL.md gains a Codex verify procedure: project trust, `/hooks` review state, three-state model, observed-hook check via `.bee/logs/hooks.jsonl`.
    23	- **D4 — Matcher supersets, never swaps.** `.codex/hooks.json` state-sync matcher becomes `update_plan|TaskCreate|TaskUpdate|TodoWrite` (superset — safe on every Codex version). An `Agent`-guard entry for Codex is added only if D2 observes PreToolUse firing for agent spawns.
    24	- **D5 — CORRECTED at validation (no action).** The "split-brain" premise was a misread: `hooks/hooks.json` is the **Codex plugin projection** and `hooks/claude-hooks.json` the **Claude projection**, both rendered from `hooks/catalog.mjs` (single source of truth) with every intentional difference pinned by the catalog drift-check + ALLOWED_DIFFERENCES (`hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs:751+`, cell codex-parity-2). Audit is codex-only, model guard claude-only, by design. Likewise the review doc's "plugin doesn't bundle hooks" claim is moot: `.codex-plugin/plugin.json` omits the `hooks` key deliberately — Codex loads `hooks/hooks.json` from the default plugin-root location. Cell cnr2-3 dropped; decision logged.
    25	- **D6 — Plugin bundles hooks (capability-gated).** If D2 confirms the plugin `hooks` key works, `.codex-plugin/plugin.json` declares it and onboarding/doctor enforce plugin-hooks XOR repo-hooks (never both silently). Otherwise repo-local `.codex/hooks.json` stays authoritative and the manifest gap is logged as asymmetry.
    26	- **D7 — `approval_policy = "never"` leaves the distributed default.** The shipped/template `.codex/config.toml` carries no approval_policy; documented profiles (`bee-safe` → on-request, `bee-autopilot` → never) replace it. Codex permission policy and bee gate_bypass are documented as distinct concepts. This repo's own working copy may keep `never` (user runs total autopilot) — distribution ≠ local choice.
    27	- **D8 — Codex custom agents unlock P25 (capability-gated).** Four roles rendered from `.bee/config.json` models at onboarding (config-rendered, same pattern as `.claude/agents/bee-*.md`): `bee-gather`/`bee-extract`-equivalent explorer (read-only), validator (read-only), worker (workspace-write), reviewer (read-only). Worker invariants (one assigned cell, reserve-before-write, no package installs, exact verify + real output, cap only on green, release reservations, single status token) go in `developer_instructions` — developer priority, not skill prose. Skills keep the detailed procedure.
    28	- **D9 — Adapter split for the 5 runtime-sensitive skills** (`bee-hive`, `bee-swarming`, `bee-executing`, `bee-validating`, `bee-reviewing`): shared core + per-runtime adapter fragments, rendered at onboarding sync into runtime-specific SKILL.md per managed root (`.claude/skills/` gets core+claude, `.agents/skills/` gets core+codex). Root `skills/` remains the single human-edited source (core + `adapters/` subdirs); no parallel `dist/` tree. Sync/mirror/release tests learn the rendering; a semantic-hash check keeps shared cores identical across runtimes. The 10 workflow-semantic skills stay unsplit.
    29	- **D10 — Advisor transport is runtime-native.** The Codex adapter of bee-executing consults model-shaped advisors via Codex-native dispatch; the `claude -p` fallback exists only in the Claude adapter or when the user explicitly configures an external Claude advisor. (This repo's own advisor is already cli-shaped `codex exec` — unaffected.)
    30	- **D11 — `bee doctor --runtime codex`.** New bee.mjs command group reporting: Codex version, project trust, hooks file present / discovered / observed-this-session (from `.bee/logs/hooks.jsonl`), pending hook review, skills discovered, custom agents discovered, active permission mode, duplicate plugin+repo hooks. Fail-closed: never "ready" from file presence alone. A `--runtime claude` counterpart reports the Claude-side equivalents (cheap once the frame exists).
    31	- **D12 — Conformance suite, automatable subset first.** Scenario harness for the review doc's 12 black-box scenarios; land the mechanically-checkable subset (write-before-Gate-3 blocked, verify-red never caps, reservation conflict blocks, doctor fail-closed, matcher coverage) as scripted tests; the interactive-judgment scenarios are documented as a manual checklist with measured metrics named, not faked.
    32	- **D13 — AGENTS.md stays under a hard 20 KiB budget, kernel-first.** Trim toward kernel + pointers where content is duplicated in skills (current 17.9 KiB, Codex cap 32 KiB shared with host-repo AGENTS.md files). Conservative: no rule is deleted, only deduplicated; every removed sentence must exist verbatim-or-stronger in a skill or runtime contract. Full 60-line kernel rewrite is OUT of scope (own future feature).
    33	
    34	## Slices (dependency order)
    35	
    36	- **S1 — Truth + guards (no new capability needed):** D3 docs cleanup, D4 matcher superset, D5 split-brain fix + parity test.
    37	- **S2 — Capability spike (read-only, disposable under `.bee/spikes/`):** observe each D2 capability on installed Codex; output: capability matrix report + gating decisions logged.
    38	- **S3 — Distribution (gated on S2):** D6 plugin hooks + XOR rule, D7 approval_policy removal + profiles.
    39	- **S4 — Native orchestration (gated on S2):** D8 custom agents + developer_instructions, D10 advisor transport.
    40	- **S5 — Adapter split:** D9 (biggest slice; regression-net-first per critical-patterns 20260716).
    41	- **S6 — Doctor:** D11.
    42	- **S7 — Conformance + docs budget:** D12, D13.
    43	
    44	## Out of scope
    45	
    46	- Full AGENTS.md 60-line kernel rewrite (D13 is dedupe-only).
    47	- Any change to gates/lanes/review doctrine semantics.
    48	- MCP server wrapper (previously deferred, stays deferred).
    49	- Claude-runtime behavior changes beyond D5 parity fix.
    50	
    51	## Acceptance criterion (feature level)
    52	
    53	A Codex session in a bee host repo gets: truthful docs, observed-or-declared-asymmetric hooks, native agent roles (if capability confirmed) with developer-priority worker invariants, runtime-clean skill prose with zero cross-runtime instruction bleed on the loaded path, a doctor that fail-closes, and scripted conformance checks green on both runtimes.

 succeeded in 102ms:
     1	# approach — codex-native-runtime-v2
     2	
     3	## Chosen path
     4	
     5	Capability-gated, slice-ordered parity build (CONTEXT.md D1–D13): land the always-safe truth/guard fixes first (S1), observe the installed Codex CLI's real capabilities second (S2), and only then build the distribution, orchestration, adapter, doctor, and conformance layers on what was actually observed. Root `skills/` stays the single human-edited source; per-runtime rendering happens inside the existing onboarding sync, not in a parallel dist tree.
     6	
     7	## Rejected alternatives
     8	
     9	- **Full skill fork (two 15-skill sets):** rejected — guaranteed drift; the review doc itself rejects it (D1).
    10	- **Trusting the review doc's Codex capability claims without observation:** rejected — the doc's own strongest insight is `hooks_file_present ≠ hooks_trusted_and_observed`; building custom agents on unverified docs would repeat the exact "file exists so it works" failure it diagnoses (D2).
    11	- **Parallel `skill-src/ + build/ + dist/` tree (doc's literal proposal):** rejected in favor of adapters under `skills/<name>/adapters/` rendered by the existing onboarding sync — one less tree, reuses the machinery that already versions, mirrors, and tests skill distribution (D9).
    12	- **Swapping hook matchers to `update_plan` only:** rejected — superset keeps old/new Codex tool names both matched; zero-risk on version skew (D4).
    13	- **Patching symptoms individually** (per-symptom fixes like "Codex reviews too much"): rejected — the review doc's closing argument; this feature is the single initiative it recommends.
    14	
    15	## Risk map
    16	
    17	| Component | Risk | Proof needed |
    18	|---|---|---|
    19	| Docs cleanup (D3) | LOW | grep-zero on stale claims; markdown intact |
    20	| Matcher superset (D4) | LOW | template-source edit + contract test; onboarding re-render idempotent |
    21	| Hook-manifest convergence (D5) | MEDIUM | new parity regression test; both plugin and repo-local install paths keep guard+audit |
    22	| Capability spike (D2) | MEDIUM | verbatim CLI evidence per capability; `unknown` is an honest verdict |
    23	| Plugin hooks XOR (D6) | MEDIUM | gated on S2; installer test for the XOR rule |
    24	| Custom agents + developer_instructions (D8) | HIGH | gated on S2; inert-config prohibition from P25 ("profiles are not shipped as inert configuration") — agents must be observed selectable |
    25	| Adapter split (D9) | HIGH | regression net FIRST (critical-pattern 20260716): freeze current rendered-skill bytes green, then change the renderer; semantic-hash test for shared cores |
    26	| Advisor transport (D10) | MEDIUM | Codex-adapter prose only; this repo's advisor is cli-shaped already |
    27	| Doctor (D11) | MEDIUM | fail-closed unit tests; never "ready" from file presence |
    28	| Conformance (D12) | MEDIUM | automatable subset as scripts; manual scenarios documented, not faked |
    29	| AGENTS.md dedupe (D13) | MEDIUM | byte budget check; every removed sentence provably present in a skill |
    30	
    31	## Order and blast radius
    32	
    33	S1 → S2 → (S3, S4, S6 parallelizable) → S5 → S7. S5 touches `onboard_bee.mjs` skill-sync — the second-highest blast-radius surface after `resolveRoots`; it gets the frozen-green regression net before any renderer change. Every rendered artifact (`.codex/hooks.json`, `.claude/skills/*`, `.agents/skills/*`, agent files) is edited at its template/generator source; the generator drift check (1.5.0) is the enforcement backstop.
    34	
    35	## Open questions for validating
    36	
    37	1. Does `codex` CLI on this machine expose agents/hook-trust surfaces inspectable read-only (S2 feasibility itself)?
    38	2. Where exactly does onboarding render `.codex/hooks.json` from (template path), and does a contract test already pin its matcher set?
    39	3. Are `hooks/hooks.json` vs `hooks/claude-hooks.json` both actually consumed (plugin route vs repo route), or is one dead — convergence (D5) vs deletion?

Two concerns are already visible: the new guard’s “unknown shape” boundary needs a stricter fixture matrix, and the documentation check currently proves less than its stated acceptance criteria.

collab: Wait
codex
Verdict: **PROCEED-WITH-CHANGES** — advisory only, not execution approval.

1. **cnr2-8 currently names the wrong authoritative field.** Codex 0.144.4 emitted `tool_input: { agent_type: "worker", message: "…" }`, not `prompt`. The guard must inspect `message`; a `prompt`-only payload is unobserved and must fail open. See [capability evidence](/home/thanhsmind/projects/goglbe/beegog/.bee/spikes/codex-native-runtime-v2/capability-matrix.md:57) and [cell wording](/home/thanhsmind/projects/goglbe/beegog/.bee/cells/cnr2-8.json:33).

2. **Use a narrow recognition boundary before denying anything.** Required fixtures:

   - Exact captured shape, anchored marker in `message`: allow.
   - Exact captured shape, mid-message or absent marker: deny.
   - Missing/null/non-object `tool_input`: fail open.
   - Missing, empty, or non-string `message`: fail open.
   - Missing, non-string, or unknown `agent_type`: fail open.
   - `prompt`-only or top-level `toolName` alias: fail open.
   - `task_name/message/fork_turns` without captured `agent_type` normalization: fail open.
   - `default` and `explorer`: fail open until their callback envelopes are probed; only `worker` was captured.
   - Extra fields may be tolerated once required fields match, but an anchored `prompt` must not rescue an unmarked `message`.

3. **Implement an isolated Codex branch.** Simply adding `spawn_agent` to the existing generic dispatch set would interpret real Codex messages as bare dispatches and produce Claude-specific model-parameter remediation. Branch early on the exact Codex envelope and leave existing Claude behavior unchanged. See [current marker extraction](/home/thanhsmind/projects/goglbe/beegog/hooks/bee-model-guard.mjs:75) and [Claude-specific enforcement](/home/thanhsmind/projects/goglbe/beegog/hooks/bee-model-guard.mjs:187).

4. **cnr2-7 is safe with lifecycle sequencing care.** Regeneration adds `AskUserQuestion`, a silent `SubagentStart` audit, and a second silent `SubagentStop` audit. The audits cannot block; `AskUserQuestion` only denies confidently malformed known schemas. The operational risk is Codex hook trust: changed hooks may be skipped pending review, and neither `approval_policy=never` nor bee’s total bypass overrides that trust layer. Complete cnr2-7 then cnr2-8, verify synthetically, review/retrust the final manifest once, and use a fresh lifecycle/session for live proof.

5. **Fold two truth corrections into this slice.** Regeneration increases the documented event set from seven to eight by adding `SubagentStart`; update [INSTALL.md](/home/thanhsmind/projects/goglbe/beegog/INSTALL.md:120) and [runtime integration docs](/home/thanhsmind/projects/goglbe/beegog/docs/06-runtime-integration.md:52). cnr2-6’s verification should also assert both profiles and the permission/gate distinction in both files—not merely grep `skills/`.

6. **Clarify renderer scope and strengthen drift proof.** Only `renderCodexHookEntries()` should gain `spawn_agent`; Claude’s `renderRepoHookEntries()` remains unchanged. Update the obsolete Claude-only allowed difference, the expected Codex command count from 12 to 13, regenerate `.codex/hooks.json` again after cnr2-8, and add explicit source↔`.bee/bin` guard parity rather than relying solely on the release manifest.

7. **Update current specs during slice close.** [hook-runtime.md](/home/thanhsmind/projects/goglbe/beegog/docs/specs/hook-runtime.md:24) and [reading-map.md](/home/thanhsmind/projects/goglbe/beegog/docs/specs/reading-map.md:69) still describe pre-spawn enforcement as Claude-only.

8. **Nothing above belongs in S5.** Keep the narrow Codex hook ABI branch, catalog projection, host rendering, and tests in cnr2-8. Defer only generalized `agent.spawn` abstraction and advisor/skill transport prose to the adapter-split slice. Also replace cnr2-8’s misleading D8 traceability: D8 custom agents were deferred; this guard follows D4 plus decision 0023.
